name: stackwarden-open-core-model
principles: StackWarden is advisory by default: it observes, evaluates, recommends, and lets teams configure enforcement.
Install and audit commands must not mutate client repositories.
Only explicit write commands may create files, and current init output is limited to .stackwarden/.
Core rules run locally and expose their deterministic logic.
Premium rules must run server-side, through licensed rulesets, or remain masked so client packages do not expose premium logic.
Secret values must never be printed in check, audit, hook, report, or plan outputs.
Blocking behavior is opt-in through .stackwarden/config.yml or an explicit strict flag.
The public online version must expose product positioning, installation, local core commands, privacy boundaries, and cloud connection points without shipping proprietary rule logic.
Client repositories must remain autonomous: core governance checks and generators work offline, while cloud features fail open unless explicitly configured as blocking.

tiers.name: core-local
tiers.description: Deterministic checks and findings implemented in the npm package and safe to inspect locally.

tiers.name: licensed-ruleset
tiers.description: Optional licensed rule bundles that may expose summaries while protecting proprietary rule implementation.

tiers.name: premium-server
tiers.description: Advanced analysis whose logic must not be shipped in the open npm package.

localGuarantees: No hidden premium rule logic in the published core package.
No blocking unless configured or strict mode is explicitly requested.

configFiles.purpose: Declares available local, licensed, and server-side capabilities plus their visibility boundaries.
Controls repository-specific advisory policy, blocking choices, and local preferences.
Source of truth for generated CODEOWNERS, protected paths, owner groups, and workspace ownership coverage.
Source of truth for package workspace metadata and generated README workspace sections.
Source of truth for affected validation domains, checks, tests, builds, and full-validation triggers.
Source of truth for enabled coding agents, generated targets, and supported agent artifact surfaces.
Source of truth for generated AI operating instructions, safety boundaries, and validation commands.
Source of truth for source-to-target projection edges, generators, checkers, and freshness contracts.

cloudResponse.findings: premium findings with masked or summary visibility
cloudResponse.recommendations: prioritized actions safe to display in client repositories

onlineVersion.name: StackWarden Cloud
onlineVersion.purpose: Provide licensed premium analysis and cross-repository governance intelligence while the public CLI remains useful offline.

mustExplain: open-core split between local core and cloud premium
install and init do not mutate codebases unless --write is explicit
secret values and source code are not sent by default
mustNotExpose: tokens, secrets, or private registry URLs

initialEndpoints.purpose: Evaluate premium findings from local metadata and core findings.
Validate token, plan, enabled capabilities, and fail-open policy.
Resolve licensed policy-pack metadata without exposing proprietary logic in the public package.

freeCapabilities: agent projection generation and drift checks
paidCapabilities: team dashboards and historical trend reports
agent-instruction consistency scoring across multiple repositories
publicLaunchChecklist: publish npm package from the public repository
keep Release Please changelog automation green
add public CI for tests and secret scanning
add stackwarden audit --cloud fail-open client path
add license/token setup docs without committing tokens
